key_insight: Danus improves long-horizon mathematical reasoning by coordinating multiple proof-search agents around a shared fact graph that stores verified claims, proofs, and dependencies.
engineering_relevance: The system offers a scalable orchestration pattern for AI proof assistants, combining planning, parallel search, verification, and persistent memory for research-level theorem exploration.
method: Main agent plans, summarizes progress, and redirects search across workers.
Multiple worker agents run parallel proof search on promising subproblems.
A stateless verifier checks claims before adding them to the shared fact graph.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports six research-level case studies across algebraic geometry, singularity theory, and combinatorics, where the fact-graph memory supported construction of long proofs.
watch_item: Performance may depend heavily on verifier reliability and careful dependency management, since any missed error or graph inconsistency could propagate through long proof chains.
paper_title: Danus: Orchestrating Mathematical Reasoning Agents with Fact-Graph Memory
